A. W. RUTAN (1880-1972)
JAMES B. TUCKER SR. (1888-1953)
WILFORD W. DAHL (1912-1973)
H. RODGER HOWELL (1925-1983)

*A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

GARVIN F. SHALLENBERGER
DAVID J. MORALDI III
JOHN J. COOPER

July 22, 1992

Jill Stecher, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Formal Written Advice on Behalf of Irvine
City Councilmember Bill Vardoulis

Dear Ms. Stecher:

This office serves as City Attorney for the City of Irvine.
On Monday, July 20, 1992, you spoke briefly with Jim Sutton of the
law firm of Nielsen, Merksamer, regarding a potential conflict of
interest situation in the City of Irvine. This letter requests
written confirmation of the advice you provided him over the
telephone, and requests that you review the sufficiency of a real
estate appraiser's report related to this matter.

For the purposes of this request, we are the authorized
representative of Irvine City Councilmember Bill Vardoulis, and
request formal, written advice pursuant to Government Code section
83114(b) and 2 California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)
regarding his duties under the Political Reform Act. Councilmember
Vardoulis' mailing address is Irvine City Hall, One Civic Center
Plaza, Irvine, California 92714. This letter contains all facts
material to your consideration of the questions presented.

Questions

1. May Councilmember Vardoulis participate in City Council
decisions regarding the development of an apartment complex located
within 1,500 feet from an office building in which he holds a
financial interest?

2. May Councilmember Vardoulis rely on the results of the
enclosed appraisal report indicating that development of the
apartment complex will not affect the value of the office building,

FS21131\04817D-0104\200337B. 07/22/92

RUTAN & TUCKER
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS


Jill Stecher, Esq.
July 22, 1992
Page 2


and does the enclosed appraisal report meet the criteria explicitly
listed in 2 California Code of Regulations section 18702(d)?

Facts

Councilmember Vardoulis holds a financial interest of more
than $1,000 in an office building located in the City of Irvine.
A developer has applied to the City for approval to build an
apartment complex on a piece of land located approximately 1,500
feet from the office building. Before the developer may begin
construction, it must obtain approval from the City Council.

The developer commissioned an appraisal of the effect of
development of the apartment complex on the Councilmember's office
building. (See copy of the appraiser's report enclosed. The
proposed apartment complex is referred to as the "McGaw property,"
and the office building in which the Councilmember Vardoulis holds
a financial interest is referred to as the "Alton property.") The
appraiser's report concludes that the apartment complex will not
affect the fair market or rental value of the office building.

The City Council may shortly be considering the developer's
request for approval of the project, and could vote on the matter
within the next month. We therefore request that the Commission
respond to our request as soon as possible. As mentioned above, we
are requesting formal advice and have included in this letter all
material facts. If you need more information in order to render
formal advice, please call us immediately.

Thank you very much for your consideration.

Very truly yours,

RUTAN & TUCKER

Joel D. Kuperberg
City Attorney

cc: Bill Vardoulis, Councilmember, City of Irvine
Paul O. Brady, Jr., City Manager, City of Irvine


PS2\131\048170-0104\203776. 07/22/92

BILL VARDOULIS, Councilmember

City of Irvine, One Civic Center Plaza, P.O. Box 19575, Irvine, California 92713 (714) 724-6000

92-430

JULY 24, 1992

Jill Stecher, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Stecher:

Earlier this week Joel Kuperberg of Rutan & Tucker, serving as the
City Attorney for the City of Irvine, requested formal written
advice regarding a potential conflict of interest that I may have.

One of Mr. Kuperberg's questions asked if I can rely upon an
appraisal report from Hall & Associates which he included. Since
then, I have received additional information from Hall & Associates
which I have enclosed, and I would appreciate it if you would
include it in your review of this issue.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

BILL VARDOULIS
Councilmember

BTV:jc

PRINTED ON RECYCLED PAPER

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or white, with only a few very small dots or marks visible in the upper right portion of the page. There is no legible text content to transcribe from this document image.

HALL & Associates
CONSULTING REAL ESTATE APPRAISERS

July 22, 1992

Mr. William W. Rice
Grimway Development Company
760 North Euclid Street, Suite 207
Anaheim, California 92801

Re: Letter of June 28, 1992 on Effect on Fair Market Value or Derivative Rental Value of
Property located at 2501 Alton Parkway. Approval of the Conditional Use Permit for an
Apartment Project at 2602 McGaw Avenue.

Dear Mr. Rice:

You have advised me that a member of the City staff has questioned the conclusions in my letter on
the basis that the letter did not specifically state that I had considered the relevant "factors" referred
to in Section 18702.3(d) of the Fair Political Practices Commission Regulations, in determining
whether the issuance of the CUP would have a material financial effect on the Alton Property, as
follows:

"(1) The proximity of the property which is the subject of the decision and the
magnitude of the proposed project or change in use in relationship to the
property in which the official has an interest.

(2) Whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will affect the
development potential or income-producing potential of the property; . . ."

While I feel that it is superfluous, I assure you that these factors, among many others, were
considered by me in reaching my conclusion, even though not specifically stated in my letter.

240 NEWPORT CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 203, NEWPORT BEACH, CA 92660 ● (714) 721-0340

***GHS***

JUL-23-1992 14:06 FROM

10

TT43213 P.02

HALL&Associates

Mr. William W. Rice
July 22, 1992
page 2

I also am told that it has been asserted that my conclusion may be biased because my fee was paid by the Chinnaways Development Company, rather than the City. I know of no professional appraiser's opinion that contradicts my conclusion in any way, and I assure you that the professional character of my conclusion is not predicated on the source of payment of my fees. Any other suggestion is unwarranted!

I would also like to add that, prior to accepting this assignment, I conferred with Mr. William Hansen, Chairman of the Ethics Committee of the Appraisal Institute, about the proper professional and ethical approach to this type of consultation assignment. It was only after said consultation that I accepted this assignment. Be assured that the assignment has been completed within strictly adhering to the standards of professional conduct of the Appraisal Institute.

I am told, further, that inquiry was made as to why I included an analysis of the feasibility of a change in the use of the Alton property to residential. I think it is eminently clear from a fair reading of my letter that the only reasonable foreseeable financial effect on the Alton property by approval of this Conditional Use Permit, in my professional opinion, would be if it were reasonable foreseeable that such approval would cause a change in use of the Alton property to residential. While it obviously always is possible that such an effect will occur, in my opinion, such a possibility is not reasonably foreseeable.

Very truly yours,

Kenneth R. Hall, SRPA
Hall & Associates

sections.question:
1. May Councilmember Vardoulis participate in City Council decisions regarding the development of an apartment complex located within 1,500 feet from an office building in which he holds a financial interest?

2. May Councilmember Vardoulis rely on the results of the enclosed appraisal report indicating that development of the apartment complex will not affect the value of the office building,

The letter from Rutan & Tucker, Attorneys at Law, dated July 22, 1992, addresses Jill Stecher, Esq., and discusses the following:

**Facts:**

Councilmember Vardoulis holds a financial interest of more than $1,000 in an office building located in the City of Irvine. A developer has applied to the City for approval to build an apartment complex on a piece of land located approximately 1,500 feet from the office building. Before the developer may begin construction, it must obtain approval from the City Council.

The developer commissioned an appraisal of the effect of development of the apartment complex on the Councilmember’s office building. (See copy of the appraiser’s report enclosed. The proposed apartment complex is referred to as the "McGaw property," and the office building in which the Councilmember Vardoulis holds a financial interest is referred to as the "Alton property.") The appraiser’s report concludes that the apartment complex will not affect the fair market or rental value of the office building.

The City Council may shortly be considering the developer’s request for approval of the project, and could vote on the matter within the next month. We therefore request that the Commission respond to our request as soon as possible. As mentioned above, we are requesting formal advice and have included in this letter all material facts. If you need more information in order to render formal advice, please call us immediately.

Thank you very much for your consideration.
